feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 - What's new?
entry.content:
SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 is now available on the App Store. Let's have a look at the new features. 

With SAP Warehouse Operator 2.4, we are introducing these new key capabilities: 

  • Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units
  • New Targeted Scan to better select a specific barcode among many barcodes
  • Support for low stock checks when confirming warehouse tasks
  • Basic validation profiles to configure required validation fields based on warehouse number and process type
  • Picking of partial quantities from Handling Units

Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units

The creation of Ad-Hoc warehouse tasks for Handling Units is now supported. If you want to move a Handling Unit inside a warehouse, but no warehouse task has been created yet, you may now use the app to create the warehouse task directly on your iPhone. Simply by scanning the Handling Unit and defining the warehouse process type for the task.

You can then walk or drive to the destination bin in the warehouse and scan it with the mobile app to finalize the creation of the task. Of course, you may also directly drop-off the Handling Unit and confirm the task.

 

Targeted Scan

There are occasions in a warehouse, when you want to select a specific barcode among many barcodes. For example, when changing the destination bin or the resource. To better guide the user for these cases, we are introducing a Targeted Scan. A finder on the screen supports the user to better aim at specific barcodes. By tapping on a highlighted barcode on the screen, it is then scanned and selected.

 

Low Stock Checks

The low stock check is a physical inventory procedure that you perform when confirming warehouse taks. Here you assume that a storage bin should only contain a small quantity of a product after a stock removal in accordance with the storage bin data in EWM. You define this quantity in the form of a limit value that is based on the operative unit of measure for the product per storage type. 

The system automatically activates the low stock check as soon as you create a warehouse task for a storage bin that should lie below the defined limit value after the stock removal. After conforming the warehouse task in SAP Warehouse Operator, the mobile app asks you to enter the remaining stock quantity in the storage bin.

 

Validation Profiles

Sometimes, it’s not needed to scan and validate all data when working on a warehouse task. Maybe the product IDs have not been printed out as barcodes in your warehouse. With the help of simple configuration profiles, it’s now possible to define which data doesn’t have to be scanned and validated using the mobile app.   

An administrator may configure the validation file and store it in Mobile Services. The file then gets fetched during the onboarding of the app and the rules are applied to the handling of the warehouse tasks. The validation profiles allow you to skip validation for product IDs and batches specific to a process type and warehouse.  

 

Additional Features

We have also enabled picking from Handling Units. However, only the picking of partial quantities is supported. Full palette picking is not supported.  

SAP Warehouse Operator now also supports Branding and Theming. So, the app can be made available to employees of your company following a consistent user experience.

Do you want to give us feedback inside the mobile app? Please tap on the new Feedback button in the user profile, which will lead you to survey to provide feedback.

 

Try the app and engage with us

Please download the new version of the app and try it out yourself.

 

 

Please leave a comment below if you have questions or feedback and we’ll get back to you.

You may also contact us at SAP_Warehouse_Operator@sap.com if you have questions on the configuration to use the app, the setup process or have specific requirements.

We also encourage you to visit our SAP Mobile Applications community page and click “follow”. We’ll be publishing more informative blog posts. Please post questions in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management area. Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Controlling internal movement of an expired Batch and blocked stock type- BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP
entry.content:
Requirement: During internal transfer posting of a Full HU (/SCWM/ADHU) or a Product transfer( /SCWM/ADPROD) using Fiori or RF transaction, there shall be a capability of stopping HU/Product movement based on certain condition.

 Business Conditions:

  • Don't allow internal movement of HU/Product if Batch is expired
  • Don't allow internal movement of HU/Product if stock type is Blocked.

Problem Addressed/ Standard Scenario: There is no standard functionality available in SAP which allows to control the movement of HU/Product based on above condition.

Solution: SAP EWM offers a BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP which can be used to put a validation on lot of business conditions. This blog will showcase a solution how to this BADI. There could be many usage of this BAdI, and primarily this is used for changing the consolidation group of WT during WO creation.( This BAdI gets triggered every time when each WT is assigned to WO).However, this is extremely critical BAdI in EWM and its usage is at lot of places before WO generation. In this blog, the business conditions of stopping internal movement of Expired & Blocked stock would be controlled using the BAdI  /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTGRP.

Replication Steps: During Bin to Bin transfer from Component warehouse to Production supply( Adhoc), system should forbid the movement of HU reading the condition from BAdI.

Using RF: Create & Conf ADHOC HU

Using /SCWM/ADHU:

 

Using RF: Create & Conf ADHOC Product

Using /SCWM/ADPROD:

BAdI details: The BAdI uses interface /SCWM/IF_EX_WHO_DSTGRP using importing parameter IT_TO which allows to read HU WT( FLGHUTO), Warehouse Process Type( PROCTY=3), Stock Type( CAT=BB), Batch( CHARG), Shelf Life Expiration Date( VFDAT< current date), Product(MATID) & Warehouse Task(TANUM). Reading the above attribute before WT creation( HU or Product), a validation is put inside the BAdI.

Other BAdIs: Customer can also use /SCWM/EX_CORE_CR_INT_CR. This BAdI is specifically designed for Internal Process and customer can put validation. This BAdI is called during every WT creation.
In this BAdI, you can read the Batch ID, HU,VFDAT, Stock type and abort WT creation using parameter setting to ET_BAPIRET. This would work both HU WT and Adhoc Product WT.

Thanks for reading. All test is performed in S/4HANA 2022 Embedded EWM.

The blog is co-authored by @prem_swaroop

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Choosing the Ideal SAP Warehousing Solution for Your Company
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain landscape, businesses require efficient and flexible warehouse management systems (WMS) to stay competitive. SAP offers robust solutions for optimizing warehouse operations. However, with multiple options available, such as Basic EWM, Advanced EWM, Decentralized EWM, and newer alternatives like SAP Stock Room Management and SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud, choosing the right one can be challenging.

This blog will explore each of these options and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable solution for your business.

Understanding the Different SAP Warehouse Management Options

1. SAP Stock Room Management

  • What It Is: Stock Room Management is a simplified warehouse management solution introduced within SAP S/4HANA. It offers core warehouse functionalities tailored for businesses with straightforward warehouse needs. SAP created Stock Room Management for customers migrating from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system to S/4HANA, but who do not require the advanced features of SAP EWM.
  • Key Features:
    • Core warehouse processes like stock transfers, goods receipt, and goods issue.
    • Basic inventory management functionalities.
    • Material handling, cycle counting, and internal movements.
    • Simplified user experience with integration into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses that require basic warehouse functionalities without the need for advanced automation or complex workflows. It is ideal for smaller warehouses or companies transitioning from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management system who want to stay within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem without upgrading to full EWM.

2. SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • What It Is: SAP Warehouse Management (WM) on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-based warehouse management functionalities for businesses that want to move their operations to the cloud. It offers core WM features while leveraging the scalability and flexibility of the public cloud infrastructure.
  • Key Features:
    • Cloud-based deployment with access to core warehouse functionalities.
    • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • Real-time inventory visibility and warehouse operations.
    • Regular updates from SAP without the need for heavy customizations.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses looking for a scalable, cloud-based solution to manage basic warehouse operations, especially those that want to reduce infrastructure costs and simplify system management. It is an excellent option for companies transitioning to the cloud or that want a more agile and scalable warehouse management solution.

3. Basic EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Basic EWM is the foundational version of SAP EWM, embedded within SAP S/4HANA. It provides essential warehouse management features with no additional licensing costs.
  • Key Features:
    • Basic warehouse processes such as picking, packing, and goods receipt/goods issue.
    • Inventory management and stock transfers.
    • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless data flow.
  • Best Suited For: Small to medium-sized businesses or warehouses with simple processes that don’t require advanced features like automation or complex workflows.

4. Advanced EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Advanced EWM is the more feature-rich version of SAP EWM, offering a comprehensive set of advanced warehouse management functionalities within SAP S/4HANA.
  • Key Features:
    • Complex warehouse operations, including cross-docking, wave management, slotting, and labor management.
    • Full support for automation systems (AGVs, conveyors).
    • Advanced stock management, including batch management and serialization.
    • Yard management, kitting, and value-added services.
  • Best Suited For: Medium to large businesses with complex warehouse processes that require high levels of automation and optimization.

5. Decentralized EWM

  • What It Is: Decentralized EWM is a stand-alone version of SAP EWM that operates on a separate server from the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, offering greater flexibility and scalability.
  • Key Features:
    • Runs independently from the SAP ERP system for improved flexibility.
    • Comprehensive functionality similar to Advanced EWM.
    • Designed for large warehouses with high transaction volumes and complex operations.
    • Multi-warehouse support and real-time process control.
  • Best Suited For: Large, global enterprises with multiple warehouses, complex operations, and high transaction volumes, where independence from the ERP system is crucial for uninterrupted operations.

Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right SAP Warehouse Management Solution

1. Warehouse Complexity

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are designed for businesses with straightforward warehouse operations. They are ideal if your processes are mostly manual, and you don’t require complex workflows or advanced automation.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer more comprehensive features, such as integration with automation, real-time process monitoring, and optimization. These are best for businesses with intricate warehouse setups, multiple locations, or advanced inventory management needs.
  • SAP WM on S/4HANA Public Cloud is suitable for companies seeking a cloud-first approach, providing core functionalities but with scalability and real-time access to inventory data.

2. Automation Needs

  • If your warehouse relies on automation or is planning to integrate automated systems (e.g., conveyors, AGVs, robots), you will need the capabilities of Advanced EWM or Decentralized EWM. These solutions offer robust support for integrating automation with your warehouse processes.
  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are better suited for manual operations or warehouses with low levels of automation.

3. Cloud Adoption Strategy

  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud is the optimal choice if your business is looking to adopt a cloud-based WMS. It provides flexibility, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure costs, making it ideal for companies focused on digital transformation and scalability.
  • For businesses that prefer an on-premise system or already have SAP S/4HANA in place, Embedded EWM (Basic or Advanced) or Decentralized EWM are viable options.

4. Cost and Licensing

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are included with SAP S/4HANA, making them cost-effective solutions for businesses that don’t need the additional features provided by Advanced or Decentralized EWM.
  • Advanced EWM requires additional licensing, but it offers significantly more functionality for businesses with complex warehouse needs.
  • Decentralized EWM comes with higher costs, as it requires separate infrastructure and licensing, but it is essential for large enterprises that need a fully independent warehouse management system.

5. Future Growth and Scalability

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are suitable for businesses with relatively stable warehouse operations that don’t anticipate significant changes in the near future.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer scalability, making them ideal for growing businesses or enterprises with plans to expand their warehouse operations, implement automation, or add new distribution centers.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-native scalability, making it an excellent choice for companies looking to grow their operations quickly and cost-effectively while leveraging cloud infrastructure.

Conclusion: Which SAP Warehouse Management Solution is Right for You?

Choosing the right SAP warehouse management solution depends on your business’s operational complexity, growth plans, and technological direction. Here's a summary of when each solution may be the best fit:

  • Stock Room Management: Best for businesses with simple warehouse operations that are moving from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system and want to continue with basic functionality within SAP S/4HANA.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud: Ideal for companies focused on adopting cloud solutions with core warehouse functionality and scalability.
  • Basic EWM: Perfect for small to medium-sized businesses that need essential warehouse management features as part of their SAP S/4HANA system without additional costs.
  • Advanced EWM: Suited for medium to large businesses with more complex warehouse operations, requiring advanced automation, inventory management, and process optimization.
  • Decentralized EWM: The best choice for large, multi-warehouse businesses that need a fully independent WMS, capable of handling high volumes and offering real-time, 24/7 operations.

By assessing your current and future warehouse needs, transaction volumes, levels of automation, and growth strategy, you can make an informed decision and choose the right SAP warehouse management solution to drive operational efficiency and support your business objectives.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE - DSC Knowledge Bits - Cookbook for Advanced Shipping and Receiving
entry.content:
Dear TM and EWM friends and fans,

hopefully all of you have heard about Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv. SR, sometimes referred to as ASR) by now, which is the new integration between S/4 HANA TM and S/4 HANA EWM. If not, I'd recommend this blog article by @Jörg_Michaelis  for an introduction and this blog article for some more insights. Additionally check out this Learning Journey and then you should have a good understanding of the topic 😉

Now, SAP have released a new Consulting Note 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook which includes a holistic set-up and customizing guide as well as a ste-by-step guide how to execute best practise scenarios. Hopefully this will help customers, partners and consultants with implementing best practice scenarios. Please note, this is a first version based on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 and going forwards the cookbook will be updated and maintained to stay on track with latest changes and improvements.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – New Migration Guide LE-WM to EWM on S/4HANA
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to share the latest updates on the new migration guide that was released for supporting customers and consultants during planning the migration from SAP ERP LE-WM or SAP S/4HANA Stock Room Management to EWM on S/4HANA. 

Additionally, I would like to clear up some confusion about end dates of usage rights and mainstream maintenance for SAP LE-WM.

LE-WM Roadmap - Important Dates:
Let's start with the SAP LE-WM roadmap and timeline, as this is mainly dependent on the ERP solution release on which LE-WM is running.

  • LE-WM on ERP ECC:
    SAP will provide mainstream maintenance until 31.12.2027 for SAP Business Suite 7 core applications, refer to SAP Note 2881788 for details.

  • LE-WM on ERP S/4HANA:
    SAP restricts the usage of components which are in a compatibility mode until 31.12.2025, refer to SAP Notes 2269324, 2881166, and 2577428 for details.

Migration Guide Overview:
In this migration guide you will find information about:

  • General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM
  • System Setup Guides
  • Organizational Structure
  • Customizing Settings for Product Master
  • Define Handling Unit Management
  • Storage Bin Migration
  • Putaway and Stock Removal
  • Material Master Migration
  • Transactional Data Migration
  • Stock Migration
  • Physical Inventory Completeness Migration

Out of Scope:

  • In general, transactional data is not transferred from LE-WM to EWM. So no documents can be open when the migration starts. For more information, see Transactional Data Migration.
  • Existing RF transactions are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • System users and the related authorization roles and profiles are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • The existing migration tools do not migrate Customizing. Due to the broader functionality in EWM, the Customizing differs from LE-WM. For more information, see General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM.
  • Customer-specific objects are not transferred

SAP Note link where you can download the mgiration guide: https://me.sap.com/notes/3523591.

Thanks to the EWM product management and CoE teams for the preparation of this useful document.

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer and you are interested in knowing more about SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Extended Warehouse Management solution, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to arrange for Architecture Point of View (APoV) service.

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Create Inbound Deliveries in EWM Using an ABAP Program (Save Inbound Delivery Replica)
entry.content:
Recently, I encountered a requirement where Purchase Order data was being sourced from a non-SAP ERP system. This data was stored in our custom Z tables within the EWM system. The goal was to enable warehouse users to create inbound delivery replicas using RF devices or a front-end transaction in a decentralized EWM environment.

As a novice in EWM system development, I was unsure about the most efficient way to accomplish this task. Therefore, I will begin by outlining my approach and the solution I discovered.

There are two standard ways I could create Inbound Delivery in EWM:

  1. Transaction: /SCWM/PRDI
  2. Standard Inbound IDoc: SHP_IBDLV_SAVE_REPLICA

Creating the delivery using the transaction was not feasible, as it didn’t align with the user’s needs. An IDoc was the only option for automating the delivery creation process. However, using an IDoc would require either triggering it via a file or establishing outbound and inbound partner profiles for my system, followed by externally triggering the outbound IDoc from my ABAP program.

  1. Debugging /SCWM/PRDI transaction: Debugging this transaction felt too complex and time-consuming, so I skipped this and went ahead with debugging IDoc function module 
  2. Testing and debugging IDoc (SHP_IBDLV_SAVE_REPLICA): The delivery was successfully created using the WE19 IDoc test transaction. I further proceeded to debug the IDoc function module. During this process, I identified a class that was utilized for creating inbound
    • Class: /scwm/cl_mapin_id_savereplica

While analyzing the function group for the IDoc function module, I discovered another function module within the same group.

/SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA: This function module, also creates EWM Inbound Deliveries using the same class and methods as the IDoc. Therefore, I decided to investigate whether I could utilize this function module in my ABAP program to create inbound deliveries. As an additional note, this function module is also used to replicate Inbound Deliveries from SAP ERP systems using RFCs.

After a few trials and resolving quite a few errors, I discovered that this function module required qRFC to successfully create inbound deliveries. Invoking this function module without qRFC would result in an ABAP runtime error or dump.

Following this, I have included an example program that demonstrates the use of the function module for creating inbound deliveries.

 

Create EWM inbound delivery replica using FM: /SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA

The following demo program illustrates how to create outbound and inbound queues using ABAP qRFC APIs and invoke the function as an RFC in a separate task.

Note: You must have an SM59 ABAP destination configured for your EWM system.

Program:

 

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZTEST_INBOUND_DELIVERY
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZTEST_INBOUND_DELIVERY.

DATA: lt_header_partner          TYPE /scwm/tt_header_partner,
      lt_header_deadlines        TYPE /scwm/tt_header_deadlines,
      lt_item_data               TYPE /scwm/tt_inb_item_data,
      lt_item_org                TYPE /scwm/tt_inb_item_org,
      lt_item_ref_purchase_order TYPE /scwm/tt_item_ref_prchse_order.


*Creating work areas.
DATA: ls_header_data   TYPE /scwm/bapiibdlvhdr,
      ls_header_org    TYPE /scwm/bapiibdlvhdrorg,
      lv_sender_system TYPE tbdls-logsys,
      ls_techn_control TYPE /scwm/bapidlvcontrol.

DATA: lv_matnr TYPE matnr VALUE '1'.



DATA: lv_idoc_status TYPE bdidocstat-status,
      lv_deliv_numb  TYPE vbeln_vl VALUE '4630900042'.


"Set Data into Import Parameters
ls_header_data = VALUE #( deliv_numb   = lv_deliv_numb
                          billoflading = 'TESTBOL'
                          extdelv_no   = |DEL_{ sy-datum }_{ sy-timlo }UTC|
                          dlv_type     = 'EL'
                          sd_doc_cat   = '7' ).

ls_header_org = VALUE #( ship_point = '0001'
                         whse_no    = 'W01' ).

lv_sender_system = 'NONSAP_ERP'.        "Define as Non-SAP ERP in SPRO configs

ls_techn_control = VALUE #( upd_ind        = 'I'
                            recv_whs_no    = 'W01'
                            recv_sys       = 'EWDCLNT100'
                            dlv_type       = 'EL'
                            internal_queue = abap_true ).

lt_header_partner = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb = lv_deliv_numb
                               itm_number = '000010'
                               partner_no = 'WH01VEN' ) ).

lt_header_deadlines = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb    = lv_deliv_numb
                                 timetype      = 'WSHDRLFDAT'
                                 timestamp_utc = '20240920150000'
                                 timezone      = 'UTC' ) ).

lt_item_data = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb        = lv_deliv_numb
                          itm_number        = '000010'
                          material          = |{ lv_matnr ALPHA = IN }|
                          dlv_qty           = '5.000'
                          sales_unit_iso    = 'EA'
                          sales_unit        = 'EA'
                          dlv_qty_stock     = '5.000'
                          base_uom          = 'EA'
                          base_uom_iso      = 'EA'
                          conv_unit_nom     = '1'
                          conv_unit_denom   = '1'
                          rec_point         = '0001'
                          item_categ        = 'ELN'
                          whse_mvmt         = '101'
                          move_type         = '101'
                          move_type_wm      = '101'
                          material_external = '1'
                          prod_date         = sy-datum ) ).

lt_item_org = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb = lv_deliv_numb
                         itm_number = '000010'
                         plant      = 'WH01'
                         stge_loc   = 'WH01' ) ).

lt_item_ref_purchase_order = VALUE #( ( deliv_numb     = lv_deliv_numb
                                        itm_number     = '000010'
                                        doc_number     = '4500000001'
                                        itm_number_ref = '00010'
                                        sd_doc_cat     = 'V'
                                        doc_cat        = 'F'
                                        doc_type       = 'NB'
                                        status         = '9'
                                        pur_group      = '001'
                                        doc_date       = '20240911'
                                        gr_ind         = 'X'
                                        gr_basediv     = 'X' ) ).

DATA: lv_deliv TYPE vbeln.

CONSTANTS: lc_queue_name      TYPE trfcqout-qname VALUE 'TEST_QUEUE_EWM_1',
           lc_queue_name_in   TYPE trfcqout-qname VALUE 'TEST_QUEUE_EWM_2',
           lc_ewd_destination TYPE rfcdes-rfcdest VALUE 'EWD'.


"Set Queue Name for the next qRFC-call
CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_SET_QUEUE_NAME'
  EXPORTING
    qname              = lc_queue_name
  EXCEPTIONS
    invalid_queue_name = 1
    OTHERS             = 2.
IF sy-subrc = 0.

  CALL FUNCTION 'TRFC_SET_QIN_PROPERTIES'
    EXPORTING
      qin_name           = lc_queue_name_in
      qout_name          = lc_queue_name
    EXCEPTIONS
      invalid_queue_name = 1
      error_message      = 2
      OTHERS             = 3.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.

    CALL FUNCTION '/SCWM/INB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA'
      IN BACKGROUND TASK
      AS SEPARATE UNIT
      DESTINATION lc_ewd_destination
      EXPORTING
        techn_control           = ls_techn_control
        header_data             = ls_header_data
        header_org              = ls_header_org
        sender_system           = lv_sender_system
      TABLES
        header_partner          = lt_header_partner
        header_deadlines        = lt_header_deadlines
        item_data               = lt_item_data
        item_org                = lt_item_org
        item_ref_purchase_order = lt_item_ref_purchase_order.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      ROLLBACK WORK.
    ELSE.
      COMMIT WORK.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

WRITE: |Execution complete for queue|.

"As the FM does not return Delivery Number,
" we can wait and fetch the delivery from table
"  using PO number as refernce
WAIT UP TO 5 SECONDS.
SELECT docno AS delivery
      FROM /scdl/db_proch_i AS a
      INNER JOIN /scdl/db_refdoc AS b
      ON a~docid = b~docid
      WHERE b~refdoccat = 'IDR'
        AND b~doccat = 'PDI'
        AND b~refbskey = 'NONSAP_ERP'
        AND b~refdocno = _deliv_numb
        INTO TABLE (lt_inb_deliv).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  WRITE: |Delivery created Successfully|.
  cl_demo_output=>display( lt_inb_deliv ).
ENDIF.

entry.title: Full and partial pallet putaway storage type determination based on quantity classification
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Requirement : Full pallet and Partial pallet determination based on quantity classification during Putaway Process with quantity determination using Packspec condition records and not based on unit of measurement attributes.

Solution Configuration:

Step1 : Define quantity classifications under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Cross Process Settings--> Warehouse Task-->Define Quantity Classifications

Here I have taken the pallets as 4 

 

Step2: Maintain the Storage Type search sequence for putaway with quantity classifications under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Goods Receipt Process-->Strategies-->Storage Type Search-->Definition of Groups-->Specify storage type search sequence for Putaway

Here I am taking full pallet storage type as RPA1 with assign quantity classifier as 4 ( pallets ) and Partial pallet Storage type as LOSE with no quantity classifiers.

 

Step3: Update the Access sequence for putaway under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Goods Receipt Process-->Strategies-->Storage Type Search-->Definition of Groups-->Optimize access strategy for Storage Type search:Putaway

 

Step4: Update the Warehouse task Determination procedure in the Warehouse number control field under the path SCM Extended Warehouse management--> Extended Warehouse Management--> Master Data-->Define Warehouse number control

I am using standard procedure 0WHTA. Here if the custom fields are added, you can update the new custom procedure

 

Master Data

Packspec Condition Records: Create the packspec Condition records for Packing(0IBD) and pallet quantity determination (0WHT).

I have taken product 100000001223 and its full pallet quantity is 100 KG

Autopack Condition Record

 

 

Full pallet Quantity determination condition record

 

 

 

Here update the quantity classifier as 4(pallets) and its respective HUType

 

 

Partial pallet Quantity Determination Condition record

Here we need to update the new condition record with sequence 2 (for partial pallets) and sequence 1 (for full pallets)

 

The min and target quantity should be updated with 1 and 99( as 100 is my full pallet quantity)

 

 

 

 

Test case: I have taken the delivery with product 100000001223 of 240 KG. Here, there are two full pallets of 100 KG and one partial pallet of 40 KG.

 

 

Here you can find the below product warehouse tasks created for HU

 

Hope that this post is clear for performing the full pallet and partial pallet withdrawal using quantity classification via condition records instead of UOM attributes.

Thanks,

Sudarshan Sathyanarayanan.

entry.title: Maximizing ROI from Your SAP EWM Investment: Expert Strategies for Continuous Optimization
entry.content:
Logistics Directors,  IT Program managers,  wonder if they are getting the most out of your SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) investment. If you feel like your warehouse operations aren’t fully optimized, you’re not alone. IT and logistics leaders often find themselves wondering if they’re truly maximizing ROI from their SAP systems. As a veteran with over three decades of experience in SAP implementations, I've seen countless organizations struggle to maximize their Return on Investment (ROI) from this powerful tool. In this article, we'll explore common challenges and proven strategies to unlock the full potential of your SAP EWM investment.

The SAP EWM ROI Challenge

Many organizations face similar hurdles when it comes to optimizing their SAP EWM systems:

  1. Inefficient processes that fail to leverage EWM's advanced capabilities
  2. Low user adoption due to inadequate training and support
  3. Limited data visibility, hindering informed decision-making

These issues aren't just minor setbacks – they're ROI killers. They result in slower order fulfillment, wasted resources, and widespread frustration across teams.

Expert Strategies for SAP EWM Optimization

Drawing from my 30+ years of experience, here are four key strategies to boost your SAP EWM ROI:

1. Harness Advanced Features

Task & Resource Management, RF Framework, Queue Management,  Slotting, Dynamic routing with POSC ( Process Oriented Storage Control) and LOSC ( Layout Oriented Storage Control),  Clustered Putaway, Wave Management,  Fixed Bin replenishment, Full pallet picking, Warehouse Monitor aren't just buzzwords. When properly implemented, they're game-changers for efficiency.

2. Conduct Regular Process Reviews

Your warehouse operations are dynamic, and your processes should be too. Implement quarterly workflow assessments to identify and eliminate inefficiencies.  SAP is heavily investing in EWM to roll out  new functionality with every release.  It is important to keep track of what is new and  what is relevant for continuous innovation of your EWM system.  Check this information at  SAP S/4HANA What’s New Viewer. Link to the viewer on SAP website is - https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5fc51e30e2744f168642e26e0c1d9be1  Please see below screenshot to filter it to EWM specific innovations.

 

 This ongoing optimization ensures your EWM system evolves with your business needs.

3. Prioritize Continuous Training

A one-time training session during implementation isn't enough. Develop a continuous learning program to keep your team proficient with SAP EWM. This approach not only improves system adoption but also empowers employees to leverage advanced features effectively. Encourage your subject matter experts (SME) to identify areas where users are making more mistakes and  focus on building focused training sessions on  those topics. These could be  scheduled as lunch and learn sessions as well without too much of training overheads.

4. Integrate Predictive Analytics

Combine SAP EWM with S/4HANA to unlock the power of real-time insights and predictive modeling. Some good examples could be open purchase orders / inbound deliveries / outbound deliveries to identify workload on warehouse. This integration can transform your decision-making  and planning capabilities, leading to smarter resource allocation and improved operational efficiency.

Remember, in the fast-paced world of logistics and supply chain management, standing still is equivalent to moving backward. It's time to optimize your SAP EWM investment and drive real, measurable ROI.

Are you facing challenges with your SAP EWM implementation? Share your experiences in the comments below, and let's discuss how to overcome them!

Shivaji Patil

SAP Warehousing Platinum Advisor

Genius Business Solutions, Inc.

entry.title: Advance Production Integration – E2E Process covering GI of components & GR of Finished Goods
entry.content:
Advanced Production Integration in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) streamlines the connection between production and warehouse operations. This integration allows businesses to effectively manage the supply of materials to production and the receipt of semi-finished or finished products back into the warehouse. This integration helps optimize material flows, improve inventory accuracy, and enhance overall operational efficiency, allowing businesses to quickly respond to changing market demands.

Advance production Integration supports 4 types of staging method – we will be covering Single Order Staging method in this blog. This blog will be divided in 2 parts – Configuration and testing.

I am using single warehouse - single storage location (AFS) for production and warehousing space in this blog.

Part – 1 Configuration:

S4 Configuration –

1. Create Material - Finished Good & Components (T-code – MM01)

First step will be creation of materials – create finished goods and components and maintain Basic Data, Sales View, Purchasing, MRP all views, Work Scheduling views.

I have created FG-11 as Finished Product and SM-11 & SM-12 as Components.

2. Create BOM for Finished Product (T-code - CS01)

Enter Material, Plant & BOM usage details – BOM usage should be 1 for production purpose. (For kitting or other operations – it will be different).

Enter components and required quantity of components to prepare finished product. The item category is “L” for stock Item.

I have taken 2 for each component to prepare our final product.

3. Create PSA – Production Supply Area (T-code – PK05)

Enter Plant, PSA, Storage Location & Responsible person name.

4. Create Work Center for Finished Product (T-code – CR01)

Add Usage – 009 – All task list types

Also, add cost center details under costing tab.

5. Routing for Finished Product (T-code – CA01)

Create Routing for finished product – it should list all the production steps.

For testing, I have maintained only 1 – 0010 as complete production step.

6. Assign Production scheduling profile & Production version to Finished Product (T-code – MM02)

Tab – Work Scheduling

7. Control cycle for components (T-code – LKP1)

Need to create control cycle maintenance for components – this will determine source & destination storage location and staging Indicator.

EWM Configuration –

1. BC Sets Activation (T-code – SCPR20)

Need to activate below BC sets, it will add document and item type related basic settings to generate PMR.

2. ERP-EWM Interface

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – INTERFACE – ERP INTEGRATION – PRODUCTION – MAP ORDER TYPE TO DOC TYPE

3. WPT & WPT Determination (GI & GR)

Create 2 WPT - I have used standard WPT - 2100 (Stock Removal) & 1100 WPT (Put away) and then maintain WPT determination criteria.

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – CROSS PROCESS SETTINGS – WAREHOUSE TASK – DEFINE WPT

4. Storage Strategies (Removal & Placement)

I have created simple strategies by using WPT only.

5. Maintain Product Master Data (T-code - /SCWM/MAT1)

Maintain control indicators if used any during WPT determination or storage search sequence.

6. Maintain Stock for components

Maintain stock for components through IB Delivery or uploading stock (/SCWM/ISU).

7. Create/Replicate PSA (T-code - /SCWM/PSA_REPLICATE)

8. Create Storage type, Production work center and related AA.

PATH – SPRO – IMG – SCM EWM – EWM – MASTER DATA – DEFINE STORAGE TYPE

Create 1 storage type for GR – 9015 and 1 for Production Supply – PROD (With storage type role – K)

Create Work center – PRD1

9. Master Data (Bin Creation, T-code - /SCWM/LS01)

Create bins – 1 for GR and 1 for production supply and then sort them by using T-code /SCWM/SBST.

Assign work center bin to work center (T-code - /SCWM/TWORKST)

10. Assigning bin to PSA (T-code - /SCWM/PSASTAGE)

Enter staging method, staging bin, staging WPT, replenishment qty if want to trigger more qty for replenishment purpose.

Part – 2 Testing:

1. Create Production Order (T-code – CO01)

Enter Finished product, Production Plant and Order type details.

Enter total quantity and Scheduling type details then navigate to component tab to see component view.

Release order and then Execute staging by clicking on Functions – WM Material Staging -Simulate & Execute and then save the document.

2. Review Order (T-code - /SCWM/PMR)

Review order document type, item type, finished product and component quantity, WPT and so on.

3. Create WT to stage components (T-code - /SCWM/STAGE)

Select any selection criteria, I am choosing order number and execute the staging, I am getting warning message as we have selected replenishment qty 5 in /SCWM/PSASTAGE t-code.

4. Confirm staging task (RFUI or directly from MON) and it will move stage the qty to stage bin.

We can check through manufacture order number.

5. Now, consumption will take place, and it will auto GI (T-code - /SCWM/RFUI)

Enter 451 to navigate to manufacturing screen directly and enter order no.

Standard screen will ask for below details – enter HU/Bin, product and quantity and hit enter to save the record.

Do this for both products, we can check the status in monitor w.r.t. manufacture order.

Verify the movement type 261 under manufacture order (T-code – CO03)

6. GR for finished product. We can use RF to create IB Del against the manufacture order.

Enter 3464 to navigate directly to receive screen by order number, system will ask work center – enter work center details and then enter the manufacture order no.

Enter the finished product details.

Next screen will ask for HU details – create HU by entering packaging material details (F2 ChPm) and update quantity (F3 ChQty) and then create HU (F4 HUCr). It will create IBD.

Review Inbound Delivery (T-code - /SCWM/PRDI), Pack Status shows completed, do the GR.

Review GR and movement types 101 back in the manufacture order (T-code – CO03)

Further, Put away can be executed in EWM for the finished product.

This is the simplest scenario in advance production integration and steps that can be followed to understand the concept of PMR, Staging methods and consumption and receiving of finished goods.

Thank you for your time and going over my work.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC SAP TM Archiving TOR Object
entry.content:
This blog aims to the simple step by step guide to do Archiving in SAP Transportation Management. This Note 2062861 - SAP TM Archiving explain lot of common issues faced.

The per-requisite for a freight order(TOR) to do Archiving is set Freight Order/Freight Unit in completed status.

1. Run Tcode – AOBJ (Transaction code to maintain the Archiving Object)

Following information is maintained for TOR Objects Archiving Object

 

Object Name – SCMTMSTOR name of the archiving object.

Write Program – Name of the program for writing data to archived files.

Delete Program – Name of the program for deleting data from DB which has been archived.

Preprocessing Program – Name of program for preprocessing of the data like validation checks.

2. Customization for Freight Order Type

In the SPRO, Maintain Archiving Retention Period in Days. This field will query the documents after completion Status for Archiving. The Archiving Date will be set to number of days plus the date on which the document is set to completed.

3. Call Transaction SARA and enter the Archive Object Name ‘SCMTMSTOR’

4. Preprocessing of Archive Data

This step is used to filter the data for Archiving based on selection criteria.

  • Create a new Variant as shown below.

  • Go back to initial screen and click on Start button. This will schedule the archiving job for Preprocessing program. In this example – I am executing it only once by choosing the immediate option.

Select the Spool Parameters based on system settings.

  • Click on Job Button to check the result data

Click on ‘Write’ button.

The button is used to execute the Write program for Archiving Objects.

Maintain the Variant and Run all the Steps as the  Pre-processing option. The result is again a spool table.

Click on ‘Delete’ button. This button would execute the delete program to delete the achieved data from the Database Table.

You can select the Archived file and set the schedule date on which data should be deleted from file.

Click the Archive Selection button and Select the TOR objects you want to Archive.

Read the Archived Data.

How to View Archive Data

  1. Run Tcode - AS_AFB(Archive File Brower)

 

Discover further insights into SAP Premium Engagement and explore how an international team of specialists can assist you in maximizing the value of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement Service can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

Kindly Share you feedback.. 

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 update. The enhancements we deliver not only further improve and compliment the SAP EWM features of the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release, but also expand integration options for EWM, such as integration with Service Order Management and extended Service Parts Planning, streamline processing steps for the Next Generation Just-In-Time (nJIT) external replenishment when using embedded S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management, Private Edition , enable circular logistic flows for returnable and reusable packaging material, offer new field extensibility options for select Warehouse Monitor nodes, and much more. The following blog post will provide an overview of the main highlights of this release, and we begin with an exploration of how this release further expands our best-of-suite integration capabilities.

INTEGRATION

Advanced Shipping & Receiving Enhancements

As with every update since the initial release of the Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration between SAP EWM and SAP TM, we invested in streamlining and expanding the capabilities of ASR. One such streamlining is the introduction of the Outbound “Streaming” process in the warehouse. What this means is that now warehouse workers can start to load available handling units (HUs) for an outbound delivery order, even if some items or HUs in the order have not been picked, packed or staged. Before the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, loading could only start when all HU’s in an outbound delivery order were ready for shipping. Now, with immediate loading, you can start the loading process for any handling unit that has the status “Packed for Shipping”. Immediate loading helps to reduce the total lead time needed for the outbound delivery. At the same time, it allows the staging areas to be used more efficiently by letting you load goods onto the truck instead of having them remain in the staging area, taking up valuable space.

Immediate loading of Handling UnitsOther Advanced Shipping & Receiving features we deliver in this release include:

  • The ability to schedule the automatic creation of waves from freight orders in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. This can be executed for both transportation planning type E (the delivery needs to be released in TM before warehouse processing can begin in EWM) and F (warehouse processing can begin immediately, and planning in TM can only be finalized when the outbound delivery order has the shipping readiness status set to “Ready”), and can be used for order-based as well as delivery-based freight order planning.
  • An enhancement to the RF loading and unloading screens which allow warehouse operators to use the container document type for radio frequency (RF) loading and unloading in Advanced Shipping and Receiving, depending on the deployment option. Please note that If you're using embedded Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) in SAP S/4HANA, you can use the container document type for RF loading and unloading. However, when using decentralized EWM based on SAP S/4HANA, you can use the container document type for RF loading only.

New RF logic transactions: “Loading by Container” for outbound process & “Unloading by Container” for inbound process

Service Order Management Integration

With this integration, users can now find an outbound delivery order (ODO) using the service order ID. When searching by service order, the system returns the outbound delivery along with references to the reservation and the service order. Users can also create a return delivery using the service order ID. Searching for orders using the service order ID improves visibility and control over service order fulfillment and returns processing.

Warehouse Management Monitor - Outbound Delivery node

The search criteria for the service order ID are available from the advanced search for the outbound delivery order and the inbound delivery. It is also integrated into the Warehouse Management Monitor. Via the Warehouse Monitor, users can display multiple ODO items for multiple Service Orders and trigger follow-up actions.

Other Integration Updates

To round off this category of updates, some more highlights that are worth mentioning are:

  • Transfer of Recommended Storage Quantity from Extended Service Parts Planning (eSPP) to Warehouse Product – with this release, users can now enable Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to take over the requirement quantity, number of order items, or recommended storage quantity from Extended Service Parts Planning (eSPP) during slotting and update these values in the warehouse product master data (transaction /SCWM/MAT1). For the technical details on how to set up the eSPP data access for the EWM slotting customizing, please read SAP Note 836899 “TDL: Setup of customizing for EWM slotting->SPP data access”
  • Quality Certificates in Goods Receipt with Synchronous Goods Posting – we have further expanded on our Quality Management integration to allow users to process quality certificates for synchronous goods receipt posting in the storage locations managed by Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA. Users can also confirm the receipt of a quality certificate when posting a goods receipt for a purchase order in the Post Goods Movement app (transaction MIGO). The quality certificate not only certifies the quality of the goods, it also records the chemical and physical properties, the inspection results, and the batch characteristics.

Activating Quality certificate process in EWM customizing "Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs"

  • Quality Inspections by Generic Article in Retail (Decentralized EWM) – one final quality management topic to mention is that now users can perform quality inspections for generic articles instead of article variants when using decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) based on SAP S/4HANA. For example, a user can inspect generic article Shirt 0815 instead of article variant Shirt 0815, Color Red, Size M. These quality planning properties can be specified in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Quality Management > Inspection Creation > BAdI: Data Transfer to Inspection Properties.

PRODUCTION INTEGRATION

Assign Multiple Bins to a Production Supply Area (PSA) for Repetitive Manufacturing (REM)

In the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 release, SAP introduced the ability to assign multiple bins for each Production Supply Area for MES-Driven staging for production & process orders, with the option to do follow-up actions, such as consumption triggered from a Manufacturing Execution System (MES), clear of Production Supply Area, etc. Now with the latest release, users can manage a production supply area (PSA) more granularly by dividing it into multiple storage bins in MES-Driven Repetitive Manufacturing. This lets you store stock separately and supply it to different resources or different production process steps in a work centre. It is now easier to keep track of the stock and ensure that the right products are available for each production process in MES-driven Repetitive Manufacturing.

Assigning multiple bins process overview

 Just as with production and process orders, users can use multiple bins assigned to PSAs for a component when working with confirmations send by a manufacturing execution system (MES) in the integration of repetitive manufacturing with Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

To implement this, warehouse clerks need to assign the storage bins to the product, party entitled to dispose, and PSA, in the Assign Bin to PSA app (transaction /SCWM/PSASTAGE2) and select MES-Relevant and Allow Multiple Bin Assignment. Then, they must define multiple storage bins for the product, party entitled to dispose, and PSA in the Assign Multiple Bins to PSA app (transaction /SCWM/MBINPSA).

Assigning multiple bins implementation example

JIT Call-Triggered Supply to Production (S2P) or to Customer (S2C) for Component Group Materials

But aside from the ability to assign multiple bins to a Production Supply Area for repetitive manufacturing, the largest block of production related improvements is the ability to fulfill a supply-to-production (S2P) call by receiving the required component group materials from a supplier to production. In some industries, vendors deliver grouped materials / components, which when received by the warehouse, are moved directly to the PSA for production. S2P stock is a new special stock category, where the S2P number would be the JIT call number which triggers the supply to production event. The S2P stock could also be posted to Supply to Customer (S2C) stock.

Here's how the S2P inbound process works:

  1. An S2P Just-In-Time (JIT) call is triggered to create an inbound delivery for component group materials in Delivery Management (LE-SHP).
  2. Transportation planning is completed in Transportation Management. Once the Ready for Warehouse Processing status is set for the relevant freight order, the delivery of Delivery Management is updated to include items for component materials.
  3. An inbound delivery in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), containing only component group materials, is now active for processing.
  4. Goods receipt is posted for the inbound delivery in EWM, and a goods receipt message is sent to Delivery Management.
  5. Finally, the put away process is done for the handling unit containing the S2P stock in the production supply area specified by the JIT call.

A Bill of Material example with Components and Component Group handling units

 To support this, we have also developed a Combined Goods Movement Service (API). This application programming interface (API) enables users to post goods movements (goods receipts, goods issues, transfer postings, and their reversals) in Inventory Management and Extended Warehouse Management. The API allows combinations of different goods movements to be posted in the same API call.

Process flow example for Combined Goods Movement Service

This API supports the following Goods Movement:

  • Combined Goods Movements
  • Goods Movements in IM only using IM managed storage locations
  • Goods Movements in EWM only for S2P/S2C stock
  • GR of HUs in EWM for normal stock and S2P/S2C stock
  • Support of auxiliary packaging materials
  • Automatic putaway warehouse task creation
  • GR of Serial numbers for normal materials/stock
  • GR of batches for normal materials/stock
  • GI from HUs for normal stock and S2P/S2C stock

Tying up this topic,, we also enable users to perform the warehouse processing steps for the Next Generation Just-In-Time (nJIT) external replenishment processes for multiple Production Supply Areas when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA. This includes the following activities with inbound delivery items for multiple production supply areas (PSAs):

  • Receipt of nJIT inbound deliveries for items containing handling units (HUs) with different destination PSAs
  • Status update of nJIT calls when confirming putaway warehouse tasks to the PSAs

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

Enhancements to Goods Distribution Process Using Distribution Equipment

Initially released in 2019 for SAP EWM, the introduction of distribution equipment supported warehouse operators in transporting goods in a warehouse to different sections in the facility, for example, components to production supply areas (PSAs). With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, we further expand the scenarios and capabilities in which distribution equipment can be used in logistics facilities:

  • Load handling units (HUs) during the unloading process - Using a radio frequency (RF) presentation device, users can load handling units (HUs) onto distribution equipment after they have already started the unloading process. When loading during unloading, a warehouse operator must specify the HU position on the distribution equipment manually. After the operator starts unloading and scans an HU to identify the distribution equipment, they can load HUs instead of confirming the next unloading warehouse task. For example, they can collect empty HUs from a production supply area (PSA) while unloading HUs and bring them to an empties buffer zone. Furthermore, users can also collect HUs containing semi-finished components from a PSA and bring them to the next PSA for further production steps.

/SCWM/RFUI Navigation: 04 Outbound Process > 06 Distribution Equip. > 04 Unloading by HU

  • Use distribution equipment in the external replenishment process for Just-In-Time (JIT) or Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls – The integration of Next Generation Just-In-Time (JIT), Delivery Management (LE-SHP), Transportation Management (TM), and Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) enables the receival of component materials based on JIT calls and to store them in the production supply area. With the latest release, warehouse workers can now use distribution equipment to move goods in the external replenishment process to PSAs. The following RF screens have been added for external replenishment for JIT or JIS calls:
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 01 Load by HU - Auto. Pos. Assignment
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 02 Load by HU - Man. Pos. Assignment
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 03 Change Loading/Unloading Details
  • 03 Inbound Processes > 06 Distribution Equipment > 04 Unload by HU

Ad Hoc Movement of HUs – Lastly, with this release we extended the API for goods distribution with distribution equipment to enable users to create ad hoc warehouse tasks for HUs to use distribution equipment for the supply of components to production. Warehouse operators can also use the monitor method Change HU Destination Data to change or delete the destination PSA and destination storage bin of one or more lowest-level HUs.

Maintain HU Destination Data via Monitor Method

Reverse Goods Receipt for Handling Units from Production

With this feature, we enable users to reverse the goods receipt of a handling unit (HU) received from production with a corresponding Logistics Execution (LE) delivery after final putaway. Since SAP S/4HANA 2023, we made it possible to reverse goods receipt of HUs after final putaway for HUs received using Advanced Production Integration without a corresponding LE delivery, where the goods receipt is communicated using the goods movement interface. Now users can reverse the goods receipt for all HUs received from production, including HUs with a corresponding LE delivery. The goods receipt doesn’t have to be communicated using the goods movement interface. If a decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) based on SAP S/4HANA system is used, the connected enterprise management system must allow the reversal of completed LE deliveries.

Reverse Goods Receipt from Product process flow

 OTHER RELEASE HIGHLIGHTS

In this final section, I would like to round off today’s blog post by mentioning a few more enhancements that we have delivered that will improve and streamline the user experiences:

  • Migration of LE-WM Data Using an XML File – To simplify data migration of LE-WM data, we have enabled users to download warehouse storage bins and warehouse stock from their legacy LE-WM system to an XML file. The XML file can then be used to upload the data to the Extended Warehouse Management system using the migration cockpit. To download the warehouse storage bins or warehouse stock, on the SAP Easy Access menu users must choose Extended Warehouse Management > Interfaces > Migration from LE-WM > LE-WM Migration via Migration Cockpit or call transaction /SCWM/MIG_FILE_MC in their Extended Warehouse Management system, instead of /SCWM/MIG_BIN and /SCWM/MIG_STOCK.

Migration of LE-WM data using an XML File process flow

  • Serialized Returnable Packaging Materials – Prior to this release, serial numbers were assigned to the contents of an HU, but not to the HU itself. As a result, the system could not automatically generate a packaging item correspondent to the HU in the outbound delivery order. However now with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, users can assign a serial number (SN) to a returnable packaging material that is used to pack a handling unit (HU) by entering the serial number in the HU header. This unique serial number helps to ensure that the correct returnable packaging material can be included in the goods issue. The serial number also allows the system to automatically generate a packaging item that corresponds to the HU.

If activated, a SN field will appear when creating an HU for a packaging material that has a SN profile assigned in the material master

  • Custom Fields for Selection Screens in the Warehouse Management Monitor – Lastly, want I want to mention is that now users can add custom fields to some of the selection screens in the warehouse management monitor. These new fields can then be used when selecting, for example, warehouse orders, warehouse tasks, or deliveries relevant for inbound, outbound, or other processes. If these Business Add-In (BAdI) methods to support variant data handling are implemented, users can also store selection values for the custom fields in monitor node selection variants.

Warehouse Monitor with Selection Screen for Outbound Delivery Order Items

To add custom selection field, users must go in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for Extended Warehouse Management > Monitoring > Warehouse Management Monitor > BAdI: Custom Selection Fields in Standard Monitor Nodes.

Custom fields can be added for the following monitor nodes and subnodes:

  • Inbound > Documents > Inbound Delivery and subnodes
  • Outbound > Documents > Outbound Delivery Order and subnodes
  • Outbound > Documents > Production Material Request and subnodes
  • Documents > Posting Changes and Posting Change Items subnode
  • Documents > Stock Transfer and Stock Transfer Items subnode
  • Stock and Bin > Storage Bin > Handling Unit
  • Stock and Bin > Handling Unit
  • Documents > Handling Unit

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics (such as examples of configuration screens), as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – Advanced Shipping & Receiving - Immediate Loading
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to explain how to configure Immediate Loading functionality in Advanced Shipping & Receiving, in addition to demonstrating the process steps for testing the happy flow scenario without freight unit or outbound delivery order.

For Scenario 2 - Partial loading with freight unit / delivery split in TM and EWM, please check the follow-up blog post - Immediate Loading with Delivery Split.

Background Information:

With Immediate Loading functionality in ASR (SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02), loading of single handling units is now allowed, even if not all items/HUs of an Outbound Delivery Order are fully picked, packed or staged, which improves the following:

  • Decrease total lead time
  • Avoids overcrowded goods issue zones
  • Allows a more flexible start of loading

Prerequisites:

NOTE: Advanced Shipping and Receiving should be configured for TM-EWM integration, for detailed steps on how to configure ASR scenario, please check SAP Note 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook.

Configuration Steps:

1- Create new Packaging Material Type (optional)
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing.


Figure 01 - Packaging Material Type

2- Assign Number Range to new Packaging Material Type (optional)
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->External Identification->Assign Number Range Intervals to Packaging Material Types.


Figure 02 - Assign NR to Packaging Material Type

3- Define new Packaging Material Type in ERP (optional)
SPRO->Logistics General->Handling Unit Management->Basics->Technical Basics->Define Packaging Material Types for Shipping.


Figure 03 - Define ERP Packaging Material Type

4- Specify how ''Packed for Shipping'' status should be updated for Packaging Material Type
SPRO->Extended Warehouse Management->Cross-Process Settings->Handling Units->Basics->Set HU to "Packed for Shipping".


Figure 04 - Define how Packing for Shipping status is updated

NOTE: ''Packed for Shipping - SPSH'' is header status on HU level.
You can configure this status to be updated either manually or automatically by setting one of the following options:  
- Manually: In this case, you set the status manually in Work Center: Packing - General or in the inbound process Package-Based Shipping from Production.
- At Picking: The status is set when the handling unit is assigned to an outbound delivery after the picking warehouse task has been confirmed.
- After Packing: The status is set when packing is complete in process-oriented storage control.
- At Staging: The status is set when the handling unit arrives at a staging area.

This status can also be set automatically if some other conditions are fulfilled, for more information please check: Packed for Shipping | SAP Help Portal

5- Set Up Access Sequence for Immediate Loading
Easy Access->Extended Warehouse Management->Settings->Advanced Shipping and Receiving->Set Up Access Sequence for Immediate Loading.


Figure 05 - Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance

6- Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance
Easy Access->Extended Warehouse Management->Settings->Advanced Shipping and Receiving->Configure Immediate Loading.


Figure 06 - Define ASR Immediate Loading relevance

7- Assign the new Packaging Material Type to a Packaging Material
Easy Access->Change Material (MM02)


Figure 07 - Create new Packaging Material

Testing Scenario and Steps:

NOTE: The following testing scenario has been executed on SAP S/4HANA OP2023 FPS02 with embedded EWM and TM solutions.

Scenario #1: Happy Flow scenario without Delivery Split:
In this scenario we will pick entire product quantity planned in outbound delivery order, then we do packing and setting of ''Packed for Shipping'' status manually for one HU out of 2 HUs and then we start loading of one HU without completing packing or setting the status for other planned HU in the outbound delivery order.

1.1- Create Outbound Delivery in Logistics Execution (Tx. VL01NO)


Figure 08 - Create outbound delivery without reference in LE

1.2- Create Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 09 - Create Picking WTs

1.3- Confirm Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 10 - Confirm Picking WTs

1.4- Pack Products into Shipping Handling Units  (Tx. /SCWM/PACK)


Figure 11 - Pack Products into SHUs

1.5- Set 'Packed for Shipping' status for one HU (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 12 - Set 'Packed for Shipping' status

Handling Units information will be communicated to Freight Order after setting Packed for Shipping status on HU in EWM.

1.6- Create Freight Order  (App. Create Road Freight Order)
1. Assign Outbound Delivery to Freight Order: Iterms -> Insert->Insert FUs Based on Base Document ID
2. Assign Carrier: Business Partner->Add Carrier 
3. Update Transportation Status: Execution Status->Set Ready for Transportation Status


Figure 13 - Create Freight Order

1.7- Loading first Handling Unit (App. Load or Unload Freight Order)
Execution Status->Set Items to Loaded


Figure 14 - Loading one HU

With the new HU header status (packing for shipping) now it's possible to start loading as soon as one HU is ready, although rest of HUs are not packed/ready yet.

1.8- Pack and Set Shipping Status for second HU (Tx. /SCWM/PACK)


Figure 15 - Packing second HU

1.9- Loading second HU (Tx. /SCWM/RFUI)
Outbound Proceses->Loading->Loading by HU


Figure 16 - Loading second HU

1.10- Set 'Ready for Shipping' Status on ODO Level (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 17 - Set to Ready for Shipping on ODO level

1.11- Check LE Outbound Delivery (Tx. VL03N)


Figure 18 - Check LE Outbound Delivery after update

Handling Units information will be communicated to LE outbound delivery after setting Ready for Shipping status on ODO level in EWM.

1.12- Post Goods Issue in EWM (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 19 - Post Goods Issue in EWM

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Conclusion:

Immediate Loading is a new feature of Advanced Shipping and Receiving that further optimises the outbound process and gives warehouses more flexibility during performing loading activities, especially in a delivery split scenario that automatically triggers the split of the freight unit and outbound delivery order in EWM and TM respectively.

During this blog, I have tried to execute the process steps using a mix of different GUI transactions and Fiori applications to show the different options available during executing each step. Also, this blog focused on a warehouse driven scenario. However, immediate loading functionality is also supported in a transport-driven scenario.

For Scenario 2 - Partial Loading with freight unit / delivery split, please check the follow-up blog post - Immediate Loading with Delivery Split.

For more information about Immediate Loading feature, please check Immediate Loading | SAP Help Portal.

For more information about other new EWM features in S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, please check the following blog post by Florian Kuchta: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS02– What’s New.
 
Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement services can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – Immediate Loading with Delivery Split
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to demonstrate the process steps required for executing immediate loading with the split of freight unit and outbound delivery order in TM and EWM respectively.

Prerequisites:

Please make sure to check blog post - Advanced Shipping & Receiving - Immediate Loading, for Immediate Loading overview, configuration steps, and testing Scenario 1 - happy flow scenario without freight unit / delivery split.

Testing Scenario and Steps:

NOTE: The following testing scenario has been executed on SAP S/4HANA OP2023 FPS02 with embedded EWM and TM solutions.

Scenario #2: Partial Loading with Delivery Split:
In this scenario we will pick and pack all items in outbound delivery order, then will set ''Packed for Shipping'' status for only one Handling Unit manually, then loading completion can be set on Freight Order level in order to trigger delivery/FU split.

2.1- Create Outbound Delivery in Logistics Execution (Tx. VL01NO)


Figure 20 - Create outbound delivery without reference in LE

2.2- Create Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 21 - Create Picking WTs

2.3- Confirm Picking Warehouse Tasks (Tx. /SCWM/RFUI)
Outbound Proceses->Picking->Picking by WO

 >  > >> 
Figure 22 - Confirm Picking WTs

2.4- Set 'Packed for Shipping' status for one HU (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 23 - Set 'Packed for Shipping' status

2.5- Create Freight Order (App. Create Road Freight Order)
1. Assign Outbound Delivery to Freight Order: Iterms -> Insert->Insert FUs Based on Base Document ID
2. Assign Carrier: Business Partner->Add Carrier 
3. Update Transportation Status: Execution Status->Set Ready for Transportation Status


Figure 24 - Create Freight Order

2.6- Loading one Handling Unit (App. Load or Unload Freight Order)
Execution Status->Set Items to Loaded


Figure 25 - Loading one HU

2.7- Set Loading Completion (App. Load or Unload Freight Order)
Execution Status->Set Stop to Loading Completed


Figure 26 - Set loading Completion

This is the trigger of FU and ODO split, so before you execute this step please make sure that you don't have open warehouse tasks for ODO which is part of the FO, otherwise error messages will appear and split won't be possible.

2.8- Check Original Outbound Delivery Order (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)
Items->Document Flow


Figure 27 - Check Original ODO

Original ODO quantity has been reduced by the quantity not loaded yet and a new split ODO has been created with the remaining quantity and HU information.

2.9- Check New Split Outbound Delivery Order (Tx. /SCWM/PRDO)


Figure 28 - Check New Split ODO

Picking and Packing statuses are updated in the new ODO with the same HU which is not loaded, additionally there is new split LE outbound delivery created of course with same quantity but without HU information as Ready for Shipping status is not set yet.

2.10- Check New Split Freight Unit (App. Delivery Integration - Worklist)
Planning Status = 01 (Not Planned)


Figure 29 - Check New Split FU

2.11- Set 'Ready for Shipping' Status in Original Outbound Delivery Order (Tx. /SCWM/MON)


Figure 30 - Set to Ready for Shipping on Original ODO

2.12- Check LE Outbound Delivery (Tx. VL03N)


Figure 31 - Check original LE Outbound Delivery after status update

Handling Units information will be communicated to LE outbound delivery after setting Ready for Shipping status on ODO level in EWM.

2.13- Post Goods Issue (App. Load or Unload Freight Order)
Goods Movement->Post


Figure 32 - Post Goods Issue

 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Conclusion:

Immediate Loading is a new feature of Advanced Shipping and Receiving that further optimises the outbound process and gives warehouses more flexibility during performing loading activities, especially in a delivery split scenario that automatically triggers the split of the freight unit and outbound delivery order in EWM and TM respectively.

During this blog, I have tried to execute the process steps using a mix of different GUI transactions and Fiori applications to show the different options available during executing each step. Also, this blog focused on a warehouse driven scenario. However, immediate loading functionality is also supported in a transport-driven scenario.

Please make sure to check blog post - Advanced Shipping & Receiving - Immediate Loading, for Immediate Loading overview, configuration steps, and testing Scenario 1 - happy flow scenario without freight unit / delivery split.

For more information about other delivery split scenarios in ASR, please check Delivery Split for Outbound Delivery Orders in Advanced Shipping and Receiving | SAP Help Portal.
